description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a green fuselage and dual straight wings, each supported by wooden struts and bracing wires. The upper and lower wings are marked with red roundels, and the aircraft features a large, exposed radial engine at the nose, with a wooden propeller. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the engine, with space for a single pilot. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted green with subtle accent lines. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft, with visible machine guns mounted on the upper fuselage.
description: A green vintage biplane with dual straight wings, open cockpit, exposed radial engine, and fixed landing gear. Red roundels and wooden struts accentuate its classic military design.
description_view_0: Front three-quarter view showing the exposed radial engine, wooden propeller, open cockpit, and both upper and lower wings with red roundels.
description_view_1: Rear three-quarter view highlighting the tail section, vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the fixed landing gear beneath the wings.
description_view_2: Direct rear view displaying the tail assembly, twin horizontal stabilizers, and the alignment of the wings and struts.
description_view_3: Side profile view emphasizing the length of the fuselage, the open cockpit, and the fixed main and tail landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or noticeable dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with exposed radial cylinders and a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine, with no canopy.
